title: Specify discovery lead
description: Validates a single raw, unmerged `Lead` block under `## Lead inventory` in `discovery.md` per workflow §Discovery handshake. Each block is one lead as surfaced by one source: a `source-key` naming the binding that produced it, a kebab-case `lead-id` (unique only within that `source-key`), a one-line per-source `summary`, an optional `tentative` boolean flag, and an optional `aliases[]` list of kebab-case aliases. Identity is the `(source-key, lead-id)` pair; the same `lead-id` MAY appear under different source keys, and cross-source unification is deferred to `/spec:plan`'s plan-time `propose` sub-step. `slices[].sources[].lead-id` resolves first against `lead-id`, then against any `aliases[]` entry, within the binding's `source-key`; aliases are stable across re-survey. An alias MUST NOT collide with another lead's `lead-id` or `aliases[]` under the same `source-key` (`discovery-alias-collision` from `specrun slice validate`).

lead-id.description: Stable kebab-case lead identifier, unique only within its `source-key`. Re-survey of that source replaces the block by `(source-key, lead-id)`.

source-key.description: Source binding key that surfaced this lead (matches a top-level `plan.yaml.sources.<key>` binding). A `survey` attributes every lead it produces to its own source key.

summary.description: One-line human-readable summary of the lead as this source surfaced it.

tentative.description: Optional survey-time uncertainty flag a source adapter may set on its own lead; the operator reconciles at Gate 1. `/spec:plan`'s `propose` sub-step reads these leads but never edits `discovery.md`.

aliases.description: Optional kebab-case aliases for cross-source unification. `slices[].sources[].lead-id` resolves a binding's lead against the lead's `lead-id` or any `alias` under the same `source-key`. Authored by the source adapter at `survey` time, or added by the operator through `specrun plan amend --add-alias`; `/spec:plan` never hand-edits `discovery.md`. Stable across re-survey; operator additions survive re-survey. Empty `aliases: []` and a missing field are equivalent.
